commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4fba0be26473db7e30037e8721e81d21ce1e6d18)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Google manifest auth alias for `google-gemini-cli`, expands Gemini CLI onboarding runtime bindings to seven Gemini chat models, and adds runtime/auth regression tests.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +419. Total +437 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level, not live-reproduced here. Current main only binds the default Gemini CLI model and lacks the manifest auth alias that `resolveCliRuntimeFromAuthProfile()` needs for `google-gemini-cli` profile matching.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because live proof cannot be supplied by automation and the model-list ownership blocker needs a branch revision before merge.

target.title: fix(google): add google-gemini-cli auth alias and expand model runtime bindings
target.body:
# fix: Gemini CLI unresponsive after re-onboarding from another provider

**Branch:** `chenyangjun-xy:fix/gemini-cli-auth-alias-reonboard` → `openclaw:main`

Closes #95851

## What Problem This Solves

After re-onboarding from another provider (e.g. Claude CLI) to Gemini CLI via `openclaw onboard`, the chat becomes unresponsive — the UI shows infinite loading and never returns a response. The onboarding completed successfully (auth tokens stored, config written), but sending a message hangs indefinitely.

## Root Cause

Two interacting gaps:

1. **Missing `providerAuthAliases`** — The Google extension manifest had no `providerAuthAliases` entry mapping `google-gemini-cli` → `google`, so `resolveCliRuntimeFromAuthProfile()` could not recognize `google-gemini-cli` auth profiles as compatible with the `google` provider. The fallback returned `undefined` and the runtime attempted a direct Google API call without valid credentials.

2. **Incomplete config patch** — The Gemini CLI config patch only set `agentRuntime` for `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, so any other Gemini model selected during model picking had no runtime binding and fell through to the broken auth profile fallback.

## Changes

### Production code (2 files)

- **`extensions/google/openclaw.plugin.json`**: Add `"providerAuthAliases": { "google-gemini-cli": "google" }` so `resolveProviderIdForAuth("google-gemini-cli")` returns `"google"`. Without this alias, the auth profile fallback path in `resolveCliRuntimeFromAuthProfile()` cannot match `google-gemini-cli` profiles to the `google` provider.

- **`extensions/google/gemini-cli-provider.ts`**: Expand the Gemini CLI config patch from a single model (`google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`) to all 7 active Gemini chat models. When users pick a model not in the original patch, the `agentRuntime` binding is absent from the `models` map, forcing the broken auth profile fallback path.

  ```typescript
  const GEMINI_CLI_MODEL_IDS = [
    "google/gemini-3.5-flash",
    "google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview",
    "google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite",
    "google/gemini-3-flash-preview",
    "google/gemini-2.5-pro",
    "google/gemini-2.5-flash",
    "google/gemini-2.5-flash-lite",
  ] as const;
  ```

### Tests (3 new tests + 1 new integration test file)

- **`src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.reonboard-gemini-cli.test.ts`** (new, 335 lines, 17 tests): Full CI integration test simulating the complete re-onboarding scenario — pre-onboarding Claude CLI config → merged Gemini CLI config → runtime resolution verification for all 7 models, backward compat, and auth profile fallback.

- **`src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.test.ts`**: New test verifying Gemini CLI runtime binding resolves correctly via `resolveCliRuntimeExecutionProvider`.

- **`src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts`**: New test verifying `google-gemini-cli` → `google` alias resolution via `resolveProviderIdForAuth`.

- **`extensions/google/manifest.test.ts`**: New test verifying the manifest declares `providerAuthAliases: { "google-gemini-cli": "google" }`.

## User Impact

Users switching from another provider to Gemini CLI via `openclaw onboard` now get working chat responses instead of infinite loading. New Gemini CLI setups and existing Claude CLI / Google API key configs are unaffected.

## Test Results

```
pnpm test src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.reonboard-gemini-cli.test.ts \
         extensions/google/manifest.test.ts \
         extensions/google/index.test.ts \
         src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.test.ts \
         src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts \
         src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --run

Test Files  6 passed (6)
     Tests  97 passed (97)
```

- **17/17** re-onboarding integration tests
- **4/4** manifest tests
- **35/35** runtime-aliases + auth-aliases tests
- Backward compat: all existing tests pass

## Evidence

- **Branch base**: `origin/main` at `ad3b2f4b88`
- **Real environment tested**: Linux Node v22.19.0 + Windows Node v24.17.0, OpenClaw `main` branch
- **Production code verification** (Windows):
  - `providerAuthAliases: { "google-gemini-cli": "google" }` confirmed in built dist manifest ✅
  - All 7 Gemini models confirmed in built `gemini-cli-provider` chunk ✅
  - `resolveProviderIdForAuth("google-gemini-cli")` → `"google"` via production chunk ✅
  - `openclaw models list --provider google` lists all 7 Gemini models ✅
  - `openclaw doctor --fix` loads 74 plugins with 0 errors ✅
- **What was not tested**: Live Gemini CLI OAuth onboarding with a real Google account sending a chat message end-to-end (requires GUI browser + Google account credentials).

## Files Changed

| File | Delta |
|------|-------|
| `extensions/google/gemini-cli-provider.ts` | +21 / -3 |
| `extensions/google/manifest.test.ts` | +7 |
| `extensions/google/openclaw.plugin.json` | +3 |
| `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.reonboard-gemini-cli.test.ts` | +335 (new) |
| `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.test.ts` | +46 |
| `src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts` | +31 |
| **Total** | **+440 / -3** |
target.author: chenyangjun-xy
